name: sec-review
description: Cybersecurity review plugin for web services, servers, browser extensions, Rust projects, and Android apps: a pipelined nine-agent architecture (sec-expert, finding-triager, cve-enricher, sast-runner, dast-runner, webext-runner, rust-runner, android-runner, report-writer) with model pinning — sonnet-grade code reasoning, haiku-grade CVE feed I/O and SAST/DAST/webext/rust/android tool dispatch. Citation-grounded expertise across databases, frameworks, webservers (including Windows/IIS as of v0.4.0), proxies, frontend, browser extensions (Chrome MV3 + Firefox AMO as of v0.6.0), the Rust/Cargo ecosystem and unsafe-surface (as of v0.7.0), Android (manifest + data + runtime, as of v0.8.0), auth, TLS, containers, secrets, and supply-chain; live CVE enrichment from NVD 2.0, OSV.dev (native coverage for crates.io and Maven), and GHSA with CISA KEV cross-reference for exploit-in-wild prioritization; SAST adapter invoking semgrep (OWASP Top Ten) and bandit; DAST lane invoking OWASP ZAP baseline against a running target URL; webext lane invoking addons-linter, web-ext lint, and retire.js against extension source directories; Rust lane invoking cargo-audit (RustSec DB), cargo-deny, cargo-geiger (unsafe-code surface, INFO-ceiling), and cargo-vet against Cargo projects; Android lane invoking mobsfscan (MASVS-mapped rules), apkleaks (secret scanning on compiled APK when present), and android-lint (via gradle wrapper or standalone) against Android source trees — with origin-tagged findings and three-state sentinels (ok/partial/unavailable) plus a clean-skip-vs-failure distinction for artifact-dependent tools; per-run token-cost accounting; offline-degradation drills for CVE feeds, SAST, DAST, webext, Rust, and Android tools; prioritized markdown report with reliable, primary-source-cited fixes.
